title: Bug: parent does not auto-close when last open child moves away
description: Reproduce: parent has status=done; add a child (parent auto-reopens to in_progress with auto_reopened journal entry per usecase/add.go:171). Now move that only open child to a different parent. Expected: original parent auto-closes back to done (mirroring auto_reopened). Actual: stays in_progress; user must corvee update --status done manually. Root cause: reopenAncestorsIfDone (add.go:171) has no companion. usecase/move.go:229 only reopens the TARGET parent, never re-evaluates the SOURCE parent. Same gap likely exists when Done/Delete completes the last open sibling. Fix scope: add closeAncestorsIfAllChildrenDone(ctx, d, startID, agent, role, now), call it from Move (on the source-parent chain) and from Done/Delete on the parent chain. Emit auto_closed journal entry + audit event symmetric to auto_reopened.
acceptance_criteria: Move: when last non-done child leaves an auto_reopened parent
parent flips to done with auto_closed journal entry
Done: when last non-done sibling completes
the auto_reopened parent flips to done
Delete: when last non-done sibling is abandoned
the auto_reopened parent flips to done
Walks the full ancestor chain (symmetric with reopenAncestorsIfDone)

journal.note: Fixed via closeAncestorsIfAllChildrenDone (add.go) + Move call site.
